name: Golden Rutilated Quartz
condition: Clean display face, light natural surface marks near base, no repairs
description: A water clear quartz crystal shot through with fine golden rutile needles that catch the light from every angle. Bahia is the classic source for this material, prized for the brightness and density of its rutile inclusions. Clean on the display face with light natural surface marks near the base, no repairs.

name: Rutile Stars in Quartz
condition: Well centered star, minor edge wear consistent with polishing
description: A polished quartz section revealing radiating sprays of reddish rutile arranged in the star pattern that made Novo Horizonte famous. This locality is the reference point for star rutile, and good stars are harder to find than the rough would suggest. Well centered star with strong contrast, minor edge wear consistent with polishing.

name: Vanadinite on Matrix
condition: Strong even color, a few small contact points on the back
description: Bright red orange hexagonal vanadinite crystals with a waxy to resinous luster, perched on matrix. Mibladen is the benchmark locality for vanadinite, known for saturated color and crisp crystal edges. Color is strong and even, with a few small contact points along the back where crystals met the host rock.

name: Chrysocolla, Botryoidal
condition: Solid and undamaged on the display side, natural cut base
description: Smooth botryoidal chrysocolla in a deep blue green, with a soft silky sheen across the rounded surfaces. The Katanga copper belt produces some of the richest chrysocolla color in the world. Solid and undamaged on the display side, with a natural cut base for easy standing.

name: Epidote on Quartz
condition: Excellent luster and clean terminations, one minor contact at base
description: Lustrous dark green epidote crystals in sharp bladed sprays, set against clear to milky quartz. Balochistan is a leading modern source for well terminated epidote with this kind of contrast. Excellent luster and clean terminations, with one minor contact at the base, no repairs.

name: Citrine, Heat-Treated
condition: Fully natural crystal form, light surface marks near base
description: A warm golden to amber citrine point with good transparency and a bright glassy luster. This is heat treated amethyst from Rio Grande do Sul, the standard and openly disclosed process behind most commercial citrine of this color. Fully natural crystal form, treatment is color only, with light surface marks near the base.

name: Fluorite with Barite
condition: Pale lavender cubes with bladed barite, undamaged display face
description: Soft lavender fluorite cubes stepping across a matrix of bladed cream barite. A quiet, aesthetic pairing where the geometry of the fluorite plays against the fans of barite. Undamaged on the display face, with natural contacts at the back edges.

name: Muscovite with Pyrite
condition: Bladed mica fans with brassy pyrite, solid matrix
description: Olive gold blades of muscovite mica fan across a brassy pyrite bearing matrix, a combination piece that reads as much like texture as crystal. Chosen for the interplay of the mica fans against the metallic pyrite. Solid throughout with natural contacts, no repairs.

name: Aragonite "Sputnik"
condition: Full symmetrical form, minor natural wear on a couple of outer tips
description: An interlocking spray of reddish brown aragonite crystals radiating outward in the shape collectors call a sputnik. Moroccan aragonite is known for these bold three dimensional clusters. Full and symmetrical form with strong luster, a couple of the outer crystal tips show minor natural wear, no repairs.

name: "Retro Rocket" Ray-Gun Lamp
condition: One of one, signed, tested and working
description: A hand-sculpted rocket and ray-gun lava lamp, shaped from Apoxie clay and hand painted, with a blue flow that reads like a jet trail. One of one, signed, tested and working. This is the kind of piece we build on commission.
